name: Chick Corea
description: American jazz and fusion pianist, keyboardist, and composer (1941-2021)
abstract: Armando Anthony "Chick" Corea (June 12, 1941 – February 9, 2021) was an American jazz pianist, composer, bandleader, and occasional percussionist. His compositions "Spain", "500 Miles High", "La Fiesta", "Armando's Rhumba", and "Windows" are considered jazz standards.

interviews.title: Chick Corea — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

works_created.t: Trio for Flute, Bassoon, and Piano

about_quotes.speaker: Herbie Hancock
about_quotes.evidence: Chick and I — I should say about when we first got together to do the first tour... we wound up actually doing two tours. One was in ‘76 and I think the other one was in 1978.
about_quotes.doc_title: Herbie Hancock interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, November 18, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: I’ll be touring with Chick Corea this summer. He’s putting together a tribute to Bud Powell. We’re going to go out with a group...
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Lellis
about_quotes.evidence: The first one was McCoy Tyner and Chick Corea... those were specifically the influences that catapulted me to try to play piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Tom Lellis interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, July 31, 2007

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: We tour every year. We’ve put out I think eight records together, and we’ve won five Grammys so far for those records.
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bela Fleck
about_quotes.evidence: Béla has enjoyed musical collaborations with artists such as Bruce Hornsby, Chic Corea and Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.doc_title: Bela Fleck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, December 7, 2004

about_quotes.speaker: Konitz Lee
about_quotes.evidence: The only time I really played with Chick Corea was in the Scientology connection at different places.
about_quotes.doc_title: Konitz Lee — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
